title: EU proposes ban on 11 crypto platforms in Russia sanctions push
summary: The proposed measures would ban transactions on 11 crypto platforms and expand sanctions targeting networks accused of helping Russia evade restrictions.

title: SpaceX IPO nears 4 times oversubscribed, squeezing crypto and tech
summary: Tech stocks and crypto are selling off in a “classic pre-mega-IPO liquidity squeeze,” say analysts.

title: Governments Sell Bonds at Record Pace as Spending Soars
summary: Governments are borrowing from syndicated bond markets at a record clip as public spending surges.

title: China Taps Commercial Oil Stocks to Help Weather Gulf Shock
summary: China has started tapping its commercial crude reserves to help offset the supply shock from the Iran war, although the world’s biggest oil importer is continuing to prioritize lower refinery use and fuel export limits to manage the fallout.

title: Japan's Largest Banks Plan Joint Stablecoin Launch by March 2027
summary: Megabanks MUFG Bank, Mizuho Bank and SMBC have formed a council to develop frameworks for jointly issuing a stablecoin in fiscal year 2026.

title: Crypto Tax Bills Face Pushback in House Committee Hearing
summary: Pro-crypto Democrats questioned staking and mining tax exemptions Tuesday, while party leadership said the bills may need to wait until after the midterms.

title: New York regulator proposes stablecoin rule to align with federal GENIUS Act, adds reserve limits
summary: The NYDFS has proposed a GENIUS Act-aligned stablecoin rule adding reserve concentration caps and mandatory risk management programs.

title: Bitcoin Layer 2 Botanix to wind down network, urges users to withdraw assets
summary: Botanix to wind down Bitcoin Layer 2 after four years, citing weak fee revenue and urging users to withdraw assets by July 9.

title: ECB moved to rein in Revolut’s ‘self-guided missiles’ in Europe
summary: Europe’s most valuable fintech was ordered to address ‘deficiencies’ in oversight amid a rapid product rollout

title: Germany’s €100bn bid to make the trains run on time
summary: The country’s railway renewal is the first test of whether Europe’s largest economy can reverse years of decline
